sanskrit: अथवा योगिनामेव कुले भवति धीमताम् । एतद्धि दुर्लभतरं लोके जन्म यदीदृशम् ॥
hindiTranslation: अथवा वैराग्यवान् योगभ्रष्ट ज्ञानवान् योगियोंके कुलमें ही जन्म लेता है ।  इस प्रकारका जो यह जन्म है, यह संसारमें बहुत ही दुर्लभ है ॥
englishTranslation: Or he is born into the family of wise yogis. Such a birth is very rare in this world.
essence: A fallen practitioner is not lost; the next birth can restart the work.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: A person who falls from yoga may be born into a family of wise practitioners. That kind of birth is very rare.
simpleInsight: Even a setback can place you closer to renewal than you think.
detailedMeaning: Krishna is reassuring Arjuna after describing the yogi who slips from practice. The one who has not finished the path does not vanish; that person may return in a family shaped by wisdom and discipline, where practice can continue.
modernRelevance: You feel like you wasted years after a relapse, a failed attempt, or a broken routine. The path is not erased; the next good environment can carry you forward again.
translationLiteral: अथवा (or) / योगिनाम् (of yogis) / एव (indeed) / कुले (in the family) / भवति (is born) / धीमताम् (of the wise) । / एतत् (this) / हि (indeed) / दुर्लभतरम् (more rare) / लोके (in the world) / जन्म (birth) / यत् (which) / ईदृशम् (of this kind)
insight: It shifts the mind from seeing failure as loss to seeing it as a delayed continuation of practice.
